title: Trouble Diagnosis — Vehicle Does Not Drive

figures_detected.description: Flowchart / check sequence for diagnosing 'Vehicle Does Not Drive' symptom on N-CVT transmission

rows.symptom: Engine races but vehicle does not drive. (Operation resembles selector lever being in "N" range)
rows.diagnosis_items: Accelerator switch is faulty | Manual control linkage incorrectly adjusted | Electromagnetic clutch circuit is faulty | Engine speed signal is faulty | N-CVT Control unit or N-CVT control unit power supply is faulty | Inhibitor switch is faulty

content_blocks.text: (Refer to diagnosis chart on following page)

content_blocks.title: Check Sequence — Vehicle Does Not Drive

nodes.text: Inspect and repair based on trouble code. When O.K. code or trouble code is not output, check N-CVT control unit power supply.
Check selector cable adjustment (Refer to "ON-VEHICLE SERVICE) | Chart reference number: 1
Adjust and replace shifter arm or selector cable
Check accelerator switch circuit | Chart reference number: 2
Check or replace accelerator switch | Check or repair harnesses connected to N-CVT control unit
Check engine RPM signal circuit | Chart reference number: 3, 4
Check or repair harness or connectors
Check inhibitor switch circuit for all ranges | Chart reference number: 4, 5, 6
Check or repair inhibitor switch harness or connectors
Check electromagnetic clutch circuit | Chart reference number: 7, 8
Check or repair electromagnetic clutch main unit, brush holder or harness.
Replace N-CVT control unit. (When replacing N-CVT control unit check that electro-magnetic clutch circuit is not short-circuited to ground.)
Check or repair: 1. Selector position mechanism 2. Oil pump 3. Control valve 4. Belt and pulley mechanism

model_notes: Applies to N-CVT (continuously variable transmission) variant of Nissan Micra K11.
